data.title: kneat signs digital transformation agreement with multinational manufacturer
data.description:
• kneat.com (TSX:KSI:CA) has signed a master services agreement with a leading multinational manufacturer.
  • As part of this agreement, the company will implement the Kneat Gx platform initially for two critical use cases: computer systems validation across its own enterprise applications; and commissioning, qualification and validation at 18 manufacturing sites within its dedicated medical and healthcare affiliate.
  • KSIOF +0.64% after hours to $3.9732.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bell Textron secures $359.87M Defense Logistics Agency contract
data.description:
• Bell Textron (NYSE:TXT) has been awarded an estimated $359.87 million modification,  exercising the five-year option period of a five-year base contract with one five-year option period for H-1 aircraft support.
  • The performance completion date is Sept. 28, 2030. 
  • The contracting activity is the Defense Logistics Agency Aviation, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

data.title: Uniti Group subsidiary prices $1.4B 7.50% senior secured notes due 2033, upsizing by $500M
data.description:
• Windstream Services, a subsidiary of Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT), priced an offering of $1.4B aggregate principal amount of 7.50% senior secured notes due 2033, up from the previously announced $900M.
  • The notes will be issued at 100% of par and are guaranteed by Uniti Group, Uniti Group LLC, and qualifying subsidiaries under senior secured and unsecured guarantees.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 6, 2025.
  • Windstream is also pursuing up to $1B incremental term loans under the legacy Windstream credit agreement, with interest at Term SOFR + 4.00% per annum.
  • Maturities of revolving credit facilities under legacy Uniti and Windstream agreements are planned to be extended to December 30, 2027.
  • Net proceeds from the new notes and term loans will primarily be used to redeem outstanding 10.50% senior secured notes due 2028 in full, including premiums, fees, and accrued interest.
  • Any remaining funds will support general corporate purposes.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: BAE Systems Land & Armaments secures $396.08M U.S. Army contract modification
data.description:
• BAE Systems Land & Armaments (OTCPK:BAESY) awarded a $396,078,918 modification to definitize the contract action for the production of Bradley-based A4 vehicles.
  • Work locations and funding will be determined with each order, with an estimated completion date of Nov. 30, 2027.
  • Army Contracting Command, Detroit Arsenal, Michigan, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Rocket Lab to acquire Mynaric AG for $150M
data.description:
• Rocket Lab Corp (NASDAQ:RKLB) has entered into an agreement with Mynaric to purchase all of the issued and outstanding shares of the German laser communication developer for $75M.
  • The acquisition agreement also provides $75M in potential additional post-closing earnout payments to the sellers in cash or shares of Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) based on revenue targets of the Mynaric business for 2025, 2026, and 2027.
  • Mynaric designs and manufactures scalable laser communication terminals for air, space, and mobile applications to enable high-speed and long-distance data transmission using laser beams instead of radio frequencies.  
  • The transaction is subject to approval from German authorities.
  • News of the acquisition drove Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares higher in after-hours trading.

data.title: Beyond Air registers 719,561 shares for potential resale by warrant holders
data.description:
• Beyond Air, Inc. (NASDAQ: XAIR) has filed a prospectus to allow for the resale of 719,561 shares of common stock by existing warrant holders.
  • The shares are issuable upon exercise of outstanding warrants with an exercise price of $2.21 per share, valid through September 9, 2030.
  • Beyond Air will not receive proceeds from the resale of shares by the selling stockholders.
  • However, XAIR will receive proceeds from any cash exercise of the warrants.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Pollard Banknote named primary Scratchers® contractor by California Lottery in multi-year deal
data.description:
• Pollard Banknote Limited (TSX:PBL:CA) has been awarded the primary contract for Scratchers® (instant tickets) and related services by the California Lottery, effective December 1, 2025.
  • The initial contract term is 6 years, with options to extend up to 6 additional years.
  • Pollard Banknote is expected to supply ~70% of the Lottery’s instant ticket portfolio over the contract’s duration.
  • Previously a secondary supplier for over 25 years, Pollard has printed many of the Lottery’s top-performing games, including the best-selling Mystery Crossword, which has generated over $6 billion in sales since launch.
  • As primary contractor, Pollard Banknote will deliver: innovative game designs and printing technologies, licensed brand promotions, retail and marketing support initiatives, portfolio optimization strategies.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Organogenesis hits setback as second late-stage ReNu study misses key endpoint
data.description:
• Organogenesis (NASDAQ:ORGO) said a second phase 3 trial of its cryopreserved amniotic suspension allograft ReNu missed its primary endpoint.
  • The first late-stage trial met the important mark.
  • ReNu, which is under investigation for treating knee osteoarthritis, did not achieve a statistically significant change in pain reduction.
  • Organogenesis said it would ask for a pre-BLA meeting with the US FDA by the end of October to examine a submission pathway, "including using the combined efficacy analysis from both Phase 3 studies to support a BLA approval," according to company COO Patrick Bilbo.

data.title: Paramount Skydance makes crucial legal chief appointment amid takeover chatter for WBD
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) has appointed Makan Delrahim as the company's chief legal officer, the media and entertainment company said in a public statement on Thursday.

Delrahim will oversee all legal, regulatory, compliance, and public policy matters, including oversight of its government relations team, the company said.

He joins from Latham & Watkins LLP, where he provided legal counsel to Skydance Media and successfully helped close the merger transaction with Paramount by staying throughout the year-long process.

Delrahim is also the former assistant attorney general for the U.S. Department of Justice's antitrust division under the first Trump presidency.

The crucial appointment by CEO David Ellison comes amid media chatter that Paramount is eyeing a takeover of rival Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD), which is planning to split into two companies by next year.

Reports also suggested that Paramount's bid for WBD would take a bit longer than expected, and the offer could be between $22 and $24 a share.

Stephanie Kyoko McKinnon, the company's general counsel and acting legal chief, will work under Delrahim, the company said.

Delrahim's appointment will be effective October 6.

data.title: Realty Income prices $800M senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
• Realty Income (NYSE:O) has announced the pricing of a public offering of $400 million of 3.950% senior unsecured notes due February 1, 2029, and $400 million of 4.500% senior unsecured notes due February 1, 2033.
  • The public offering price for the 2029 notes was 99.412% of the principal amount for an effective yield to maturity of 4.143%, and the public offering price for the 2033 notes was 98.871% of the principal amount for an effective yield to maturity of 4.685%. Combined, the notes have a weighted average tenor of about 5.3 years and a weighted average yield to maturity of 4.414%.
  • This offering is expected to close on October 6, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
